label: Multiple myeloma
quick_take: Multiple myeloma has the largest persistent US Black excess of any haematological cancer — Black ASR sits at 2–4× the US White rate across every volume from III to XII. The signal is stable, not narrowing. Elsewhere the story is a universal low-level rise, largely driven by better serum-electrophoresis diagnosis.

findings.title: USA San Francisco Bay Black men, vol III: ASR 11.64, 5× the world median
findings.text: Nebraska Black women vol XII 12.19 (4.3×). New Zealand Māori men vol II 8.82. Multiple myeloma's Black-population excess shows up as early as vol III and is still at the top of US-male rankings in vol XII.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — USA San Francisco Bay Black men, vol III: ASR 11.64, 5× the world median

findings.title: USA ethnic contrast inside the same country reaches 26× in vol III
findings.text: (SF Bay Black 11.64 vs Hispanic 0.44) — narrower but still 12–17× in vol X.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — USA ethnic contrast inside the same country reaches 26× in vol III

findings.title: New Zealand Pacific peoples women: inverted-U 2.10 → 8.90 (vol XI) → 8.34
findings.text: Pacific subgroup n=2→60 cases (SMALL_N baseline) + pop_warning NZ Pacific vol X. The inverted-U shape is directionally sound but the exact peak height should be treated cautiously. A Pacific-peoples myeloma excess that has not received much attention.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — New Zealand Pacific peoples women: inverted-U 2.10 → 8.90 (vol XI) → 8.34

findings.title: The "universal rise" is the cleanest haematological trend in CI5
findings.text: Virtually no registry shows a decline; most are up 2–6×. Extreme %-changes (Miyagi women +3125 %, Algeria Sétif men +1356 %) are driven by baselines of 1–4 cases; absolute final ASRs (2–5 per 100 000) are modest. The *direction* is universal and robust, but headline percentages should be reported with their baseline case counts.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — The "universal rise" is the cleanest haematological trend in CI5

findings.title: Uganda Kyadondo men 0.66 → 2.72, U-shape
findings.text: African rise visible on top of the universal one.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — Uganda Kyadondo men 0.66 → 2.72, U-shape

findings.title: No cancer_warning, no bimodal or early-peak anomalies
findings.text: myeloma's age distribution is a clean unimodal older-adult peak everywhere.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — No cancer_warning, no bimodal or early-peak anomalies

findings.text: Much of the universal rise reflects better serum electrophoresis and bone-marrow biopsy rates over time. Some is real.
Small numbers in early volumes for non-US registries exaggerate % changes.

findings.title: The persistent US Black × myeloma excess is arguably the most important persistent racial disparity in haematology, and CI5 XII shows it still at 12–15× — worth a focused paper
findings.text: **The persistent US Black × myeloma excess is arguably the most important persistent racial disparity in haematology, and CI5 XII shows it still at 12–15× — worth a focused paper.**
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — The persistent US Black × myeloma excess is arguably the most important persistent racial disparity in haematology, and CI5 XII shows it still at 12–15× — worth a focused paper

findings.title: The Pacific-peoples (Hawaiian, NZ Māori, NZ Pacific) myeloma excess
findings.text: is under-described and could be packaged as a coherent indigenous-Pacific story.
findings.image_alt: CI5 trend chart — The Pacific-peoples (Hawaiian, NZ Māori, NZ Pacific) myeloma excess

findings.text: Decompose the worldwide rise into a "diagnostic access" component (pre-vol-VIII) and a "real rise" component (post-vol-VIII) by fitting piecewise trends.
